$comment: Single source of truth for POMA Grill MCP tool definitions. Consumed directly by the Node implementation; the Go implementation (go/tools/grill.go) currently maintains hand-written copies that must be kept in sync until codegen lands. See PLAN.md.

tools.name: grill_explain
tools.description: Returns a structured explanation of how POMA Grill works: ingesting data, searching, result format, and how to get an API key. No arguments or authentication required.

tools.name: grill_ingest
tools.description: Ingest a file into POMA Grill (context engine). Provide exactly one of file_path (large/local) or file_base64 (small). Returns job_id. Once done, doc_id equals job_id for grill_search. Backpressure: if the response has retryable=true (too_many_jobs — account at concurrent-job capacity), the document was NOT ingested; wait retry_after_seconds and retry the SAME call, and pause new ingests until capacity frees rather than retrying in a tight loop.

file_base64.description: Standard base64 of the file bytes. Use for small files. Mutually exclusive with file_path.

file_path.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path readable by the MCP server process (local stdio). Preferred for large files. Mutually exclusive with file_base64. Optional env: GRILL_INGEST_ALLOWED_PREFIX, GRILL_INGEST_MAX_BYTES.

filename.description: Original basename (e.g. report.pdf). Optional; inferred from file_path or content when possible.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

project_id.description: Project ID to use when authenticating with an account-level API key. Not needed with a project API key. Falls back to POMA_PROJECT_ID env var.

retryable.description: True when the error is transient job-capacity backpressure (too_many_jobs): the ingest did not happen; retry the same call after retry_after_seconds and pause new ingests until capacity frees.

retry_after_seconds.description: Suggested seconds to wait before retrying when retryable is true.

tools.name: grill_ingest_sync
tools.description: Ingest a file into POMA Grill; waits until terminal state. Provide exactly one of file_path (large/local) or file_base64 (small). Returns job_id and status events. Backpressure: if the response has retryable=true (too_many_jobs — account at concurrent-job capacity), the document was NOT ingested; wait retry_after_seconds and retry the SAME call, and pause new ingests until capacity frees.

file_base64.description: Standard base64 of the file bytes. Use for small files. Mutually exclusive with file_path.

file_path.description: Absolute or cwd-relative path readable by the MCP server process (local stdio). Preferred for large files. Mutually exclusive with file_base64. Optional env: GRILL_INGEST_ALLOWED_PREFIX, GRILL_INGEST_MAX_BYTES.

filename.description: Original basename (e.g. report.pdf). Optional; inferred from file_path or content when possible.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

project_id.description: Project ID to use when authenticating with an account-level API key. Not needed with a project API key. Falls back to POMA_PROJECT_ID env var.

retryable.description: True when the error is transient job-capacity backpressure (too_many_jobs): the ingest did not happen; retry the same call after retry_after_seconds and pause new ingests until capacity frees.

retry_after_seconds.description: Suggested seconds to wait before retrying when retryable is true.

tools.name: grill_ingest_resume
tools.description: Resume tracking an in-progress POMA Grill ingestion job. Connects to the status SSE stream for the given job_id and waits until a terminal state (done, failed, grilled, deleted), emitting progress notifications. Use when grill_ingest was called earlier and you need to wait for completion.

job_id.description: Job ID returned by a previous grill_ingest call.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

tools.name: grill_ingest_batch
tools.description: Ingest multiple files into POMA Grill with controlled upload concurrency (default 5, max 10). Accepts up to 50 file paths. Returns job_ids immediately after uploads complete — does not wait for server-side processing. Use grill_jobs_status to monitor progress. On free-tier accounts use concurrency:1. When the account is at its concurrent-job capacity the API returns HTTP 429 too_many_jobs; those files come back with quota_exceed=true (counted in quota_exceeded_count) — they were NOT ingested. Retry only the quota_exceed files once running jobs finish (poll grill_jobs_status); lower concurrency if it recurs.

file_paths.description: Absolute or cwd-relative paths readable by the MCP server process. Max 50 files.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

concurrency.description: Upload concurrency (default 5, max 10). Use 1 for free-tier accounts.

project_id.description: Project ID to use when authenticating with an account-level API key. Not needed with a project API key. Falls back to POMA_PROJECT_ID env var.

tools.name: grill_jobs_status
tools.description: Get current status for one or more POMA Grill jobs (up to 50). Returns a JSON snapshot per job — no streaming. Use periodically after grill_ingest or grill_ingest_batch to check progress. pending_count/done_count/failed_count give a quick summary.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

tools.name: grill_search
tools.description: Search the POMA Grill context engine and return a context block for RAG. Set doc_filter to restrict search to a specific document (use the job_id returned by grill_ingest as the doc_id). Use exclude_doc_ids to skip docs already cited in this conversation. Result count is bounded server-side by relevance and a token budget — there is no top_k.

doc_filter.description: Optional doc_id to restrict search to a single document. When set, routes to the searchInDoc endpoint.

exclude_doc_ids.description: Doc ids to exclude from results. Useful in agent loops to avoid re-citing docs already shown. Max 100.

return_assets.description: Return the cited documents' figures/tables in the `assets` output field (keyed by doc_id; images are base64 data URIs).

return_page_images.description: DEPRECATED / not available — no-op today. Full-page screenshots are not returned inline.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

project_id.description: Project ID to use when authenticating with an account-level API key. Not needed with a project API key. Falls back to POMA_PROJECT_ID env var.

context.description: Concatenated chunk text for RAG prompting.

assets.description: Per-doc figures/tables when return_assets=true and at least one cited document has them, keyed by doc_id. Images are base64 data URIs. Absent when no cited document has assets (the API may send null or omit the field; both are treated as absent here).

tools.name: grill_docs_list
tools.description: List documents currently ingested into POMA Grill for the authenticated project namespace. Returns metadata only (doc_id, filename, ingested_at, chunk/page counts, etc.) — use grill_search to retrieve content. Pass a returned doc_id as doc_filter on grill_search to scope a query to a specific document.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

project_id.description: Project ID to use when authenticating with an account-level API key. Not needed with a project API key. Falls back to POMA_PROJECT_ID env var.

tools.name: grill_projects
tools.description: List your accessible projects. Returns project IDs, names, product types, and protection status. Use this to find the project_id for a project by name before calling other Grill tools.

token.description: POMA API JWT. Usually not needed — the server inherits the token from the Authorization header in the MCP client config or the POMA_API_KEY env var. Only pass explicitly to override.

product.description: Filter by product type. If omitted, returns all projects.

projects.description: Human-readable list of accessible projects with IDs, names, products, and protection status.
